name: Capri Holdings Ltd
business: Capri Holdings Limited is a global fashion luxury group consisting of iconic brands Michael Kors and Jimmy Choo, selling accessories, footwear, apparel, and licensed products. The company sells through Company-operated retail stores and e-commerce sites, leading department stores, specialty stores, and select licensing partners in over 100 countries.
sellsTo: Department stores (Bloomingdale's, Dillard's, Macy's, Nordstrom, Saks Fifth Avenue, Galeries Lafayette, Harrods, Harvey Nichols, Printemps, Selfridges)
namedSuppliers: one third-party buying agent sourced approximately 14% of Michael Kors finished goods purchases

Michael Kors largest manufacturing contractor, who produces its products in Asia and who Michael Kors has worked with for over 20 years, accounted for the production of approximately 14% of its finished products
owned Italian ateliers and footwear manufacturers

exposures.sentence: E-commerce represents approximately 21% of our net revenues and has been our fastest growing business over the last several years.
Privacy breaches and other cybersecurity risks related to our business could negatively affect our reputation, credibility and business.

Such initiatives include the use of artificial intelligence and other advanced technologies (AI) and machine learning, which we have started to incorporate into our systems and processes in Fiscal 2026 to support data-driven insights and internal planning processes.

tamClaims.claim: In 2025, the personal luxury goods market is estimated to have declined modestly to approximately 358 billion.
tamClaims.sentence: In 2025, the personal luxury goods market is estimated to have declined modestly to approximately 358 billion.

tamClaims.claim: Bain forecasts the personal luxury goods market will grow at a 4% to 6% compound annual growth rate, reaching approximately 525 to 640 billion by 2035.
tamClaims.sentence: Over the long term, Bain forecasts the personal luxury goods market will grow at a 4% to 6% compound annual growth rate, reaching approximately 525 to 640 billion by 2035, driven by an expanding addressable consumer base, global wealth creation and increased participation from younger consumers.
